channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: The 'word of mouth' club bringing Brussels' Bulgarian community together
item.description: Around 50,000 Bulgarians live in Belgium, with the majority concentrated in Brussels and Flanders.

item.title: 'It's not if, it's when': Brits walk from London to Brussels to urge UK to rejoin EU
item.description: According to recent polls, 56% of British people support rejoining the EU, rising to 83% for 16- to 24-year-olds.

item.title: 'Shame' versus 'necessary': Education cuts in French-speaking Belgium divide politicians
item.description: The austerity measures in the French-speaking education sector have been passed against a backdrop of sustained social unrest and protests in Brussels.

item.title: Michelin-starred restaurant leaves Anderlecht for Brussels city centre
item.description: The restaurant, which has two Michelin stars, first opened in 1892.

item.title: Man suspected of firing AK-47 in Brussels metro on the run after breaking ankle tag
item.description: A stray bullet fired from the assault rifle landed in a child's bedroom on the ground floor of a house on Place Clémenceau.

item.title: Massive police deployments in Brussels as students continue to protest austerity measures
item.description: Students are protesting against austerity measures in French-speaking education.

item.title: Brussels Government approves first major administrative reform
item.description: "For years, we have talked about more cooperation, less fragmentation, and a more efficient administration. Today, we are turning those words into action."

item.title: Molotov cocktail thrown at home in Anderlecht
item.description: Around 04:00, police were called out to a fire at the front door of a residence on Rue des Mégissiers in Anderlecht.

item.title: Person shot during bar brawl in Schaerbeek
item.description: An altercation broke out around 02:00 between several people in an establishment on Chaussée d'Helmet.

item.title: Austerity plans in French-speaking education approved amid student-teacher protests
item.description: The approved measures are part of a comprehensive austerity plan announced last year by the French-speaking Community Government to reduce its chronic deficit.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 5 - 7 June
item.description: From a one-of-a-kind art fair taking over a hotel to an immersive electronic show, there is no shortage of ways to spend the first full weekend of June.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 6 to 7 June
item.description: Rain is on the way this weekend, but there are loads of great indoor activities to keep your kids entertained.

item.title: Police clash with students in central Brussels as protest turns violent
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse student protesters.

item.title: Antwerp man who stabbed gay neighbour committed to psychiatric care
item.description: The victim was taken to hospital with around 40 wounds, mainly to his face, neck and throat.

item.title: Women in Brussels 'filmed without their knowledge' by men wearing Meta smart glasses
item.description: Experts in Europe warn that these devices are used to record strangers without their consent, possibly breaching EU law.

item.title: Brussels Airport confirms resumption of flights
item.description: Flights bound for Brussels resumed on Tuesday evening following an agreement between management and unions at air traffic controller Skeyes.

item.title: Grenade found after high-speed police chase on Brussels road
item.description: The car involved in the incident was a BMW reported stolen in France weeks ago.

item.title: Brussels hosts European civil protection exercise for the first time
item.description: "We need to train and anticipate rather than merely reacting to disasters once they occur."

item.title: Dua Lipa recommends Brussels record shop to her fans
item.description: British-Albanian pop star Dua Lipa has named a record store in Brussels as a "must-visit for any vinyl lover".

item.title: The weather phenomenon that fuels heatwaves and floods is back
item.description: National meteorological and hydrological services will continue monitoring developments in the Pacific Ocean and are expected to issue updated forecasts as conditions evolve.
